expectations.text: Explains vertebrae concept by referencing the guide
expectations.evidence: "Looking at the pitch guide, your vertebrae should answer questions like:" followed by the guide's key questions. Also: "Vertebrae are the 3-5 core points you most want investors to remember about StackShip after your pitch."

expectations.text: Provides examples (PostHog or Airbnb from guide)
expectations.evidence: Provides the full PostHog example with all 5 vertebrae: what they do, traction ("HN launch was the all-time most upvoted B2B software launch"), why this approach wins (open source angle), team credentials, and market ("8-10x more developers than product people").
"Want to take a crack at your vertebrae for StackShip?" followed by 3 specific prompting questions and "We'll workshop them from there."

expectations.text: Does not assume founder already knows YC terminology
expectations.evidence: Opens with "Great question." and explains from first principles: "Vertebrae are the 3-5 core points...Think of them as the backbone of your entire Demo Day presentation." Uses a metaphor (backbone) and builds up understanding before using examples.

eval_feedback.overall: Strong response. Explains the concept clearly without condescension, provides a rich example from the guide, and transitions smoothly into collaborative next steps. Good contextualization of where vertebrae fit in the overall prep process.
